methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: The Bot Company
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B)

data.narrative:
Freeform, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Erik Palitsch, has raised $125M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public entity operating in the same sector at Series B and adjacent stages, including peers such as Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Hadrian, and Nimble Robotics.

Two triangulation methods apply to this analysis. The stage-progression method, anchoring to $125M total raised as a proxy for entry valuation and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, implies a valuation of $238M. The peer-median method references a cohort of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.8B, yielding a median of $1.6B.

The resulting valuation range is $202M to $1.9B, with a median estimate of $931M. This wide spread reflects material tension between early-stage capitalization benchmarks and late-stage peer valuations at Series B/C. The stage-progression method likely understates current value given the company's capital raise size relative to cohort, while the peer median may incorporate outliers given the small sample. Key uncertainties include Freeform's relative progress on technical and commercial milestones versus named peers, and whether the peer set fully reflects stage-specific risk profiles.
